steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is 'The Proto-Atlantic-Congo language' (singular). The appositive phrase that follows renames it but does not change its number. The verb must agree with a singular subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "are": "Are" is a plural verb and does not agree with the singular subject "The Proto-Atlantic-Congo language." ✗
  • Choice B — "is": "Is" is the singular present-tense form that correctly agrees with the singular subject. ✓
  • Choice C — "have been": "Have been" is a plural perfect form that does not satisfy singular subject-verb agreement. ✗
  • Choice D — "were": "Were" is a past-tense plural form that also fails to match the singular subject. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
